proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with npm1_mutation=0.
Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with tp53_mutation=0.
Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower objective_response rate.

Patients labeled unfit_for_intensive=1 have a higher objective_response rate than those with unfit_for_intensive=0 (in this cohort, likely because they are preferentially given venetoclax-azacitidine).
Patients with flt3_itd=1 have an objective_response rate that differs from flt3_itd=0.
Patients with idh1_mutation=1 have an objective_response rate that differs from idh1_mutation=0.
Patients with idh2_mutation=1 have an objective_response rate that differs from idh2_mutation=0.
Sex_female is associated with objective_response.
secondary_aml=1 is associated with a lower objective_response rate than secondary_aml=0.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 23.46% in npm1+ vs 16.73% in npm1- (n=15014 vs 34986); chi-square p=5.8e-70.
ORR 16.57% in tp53+ vs 18.99% in tp53- (n=4985 vs 45015); chi-square p=3.5e-5.
ORR 16.81% with CK vs 19.09% without CK (n=7462 vs 42538); chi-square p=3.3e-6.
ORR 22.10% in unfit vs 16.51% in fit (n=20056 vs 29944); chi-square p=1.9e-55.
ORR 18.92% in flt3_itd+ vs 18.71% in flt3_itd- (n=10000 vs 40000); chi-square p=0.64. No main effect.
ORR 19.09% in idh1+ vs 18.72% in idh1-; p=0.60. No main effect.
ORR 19.15% in idh2+ vs 18.71% in idh2-; p=0.47. No main effect.
ORR 18.91% female vs 18.62% male; p=0.42. No main effect.
ORR 18.81% secondary vs 18.73% de novo; p=0.85. No main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher nlr is associated with lower objective_response.
Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean WBC 19.27 (responders) vs 20.39 (non-responders); Welch t-test p=1.5e-4.
Mean albumin 3.824 vs 3.795 g/dL; p=2.7e-7.
Mean weight loss 3.44% vs 3.95%; p=1.7e-33.
Mean CRP 5.69 vs 6.17 mg/L; p=1.6e-6.
Mean NLR 3.50 vs 3.50; p=0.92. No main effect.
Mean age 67.98 vs 68.00; p=0.88. No main effect.
Mean LDH 223.1 vs 224.0; p=0.35. No main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 is associated with a higher objective_response rate than =0.
treatment_7plus3=1 is associated with a higher objective_response rate than =0.
treatment_midostaurin=1 is associated with a different objective_response rate than =0.
treatment_gilteritinib=1 is associated with a different objective_response rate than =0.
treatment_ivosidenib=1 is associated with a different objective_response rate than =0.
treatment_enasidenib=1 is associated with a different objective_response rate than =0.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 21.96% on ven+aza vs 16.61% off; p=6.6e-51. Largest treatment-level main effect.
ORR 18.89% on 7+3 vs 18.63% off; p=0.46. No marginal main effect.
ORR 18.57% on midostaurin vs 18.78% off; p=0.67. No marginal main effect.
ORR 18.13% on gilteritinib vs 18.86% off; p=0.14. No marginal main effect.
ORR 18.82% on ivosidenib vs 18.74% off; p=0.93. No marginal main effect.
ORR 18.58% on enasidenib vs 18.76% off; p=0.79. No marginal main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, secondary AML, unfit, complex karyotype, mutations, blood counts, and lab markers, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remains positively associated with objective_response.
After multivariable adjustment, npm1_mutation remains a strong positive predictor and tp53_mutation/complex_karyotype remain negative predictors.
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_midostaurin/gilteritinib/ivosidenib/enasidenib/7+3 each show no independent main effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ven+aza beta=+0.361 (OR=1.43), z=15.4, p<1e-50. Confirmed strong positive effect on log-odds scale.
Adjusted betas: midostaurin -0.010 (p=0.76), gilteritinib -0.049 (p=0.13), ivosidenib +0.019 (p=0.66), enasidenib -0.010 (p=0.82), 7+3 +0.015 (p=0.52). None significant on objective_response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response specifically in flt3_itd=1 patients (positive treatment-by-flt3_itd interaction).
treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response specifically in flt3_tkd=1 patients (positive treatment-by-flt3_tkd interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.106, p=0.19. In flt3_itd+: ORR 17.64% on tx vs 19.14% off (diff=-0.015, p=0.19). No evidence midostaurin helps flt3_itd+; numerical trend opposite to expected.
Interaction beta=-0.023, p=0.87. No effect modification by flt3_tkd.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response specifically in flt3_itd=1 patients.
treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response specifically in flt3_tkd=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.079, p=0.32. In flt3_itd+: ORR 19.11% on gilteritinib vs 18.89% off (diff=+0.002). No meaningful effect modification.
Interaction beta=-0.213, p=0.16. In flt3_tkd+: ORR 15.07% on gilteritinib vs 18.56% off (diff=-0.035, n=418/2397). No significant effect modification (point estimate trends opposite to expected).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response specifically in idh1_mutation=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.304, p=0.077. In idh1+: ORR 15.44% on ivosidenib vs 19.42% off (diff=-0.040, n=298/3326); in idh1-: diff +0.004. Borderline trend opposite to expected target effect; not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response specifically in idh2_mutation=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.039, p=0.79. In idh2+: ORR 19.49% on enasidenib vs 19.12% off (diff=+0.004). No effect modification by idh2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a positive effect on objective_response that is concentrated in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients (positive ven+aza × unfit interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.883, z=18.78, p=1.1e-78. In unfit: ORR 30.81% on ven+aza vs 16.30% off (diff=+0.145, p=7e-130). In fit: diff -0.008 (p=0.08). Strong positive effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_7plus3 has a positive effect on objective_response that is larger in fit (unfit_for_intensive=0) patients than in unfit patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.027, p=0.56. In fit: 7+3 diff +0.004 (p=0.38); in unfit: diff +0.000 (p=0.99). 7+3 shows essentially no effect on ORR in either stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1, the ven+aza ORR benefit is concentrated in npm1_mutation=1 patients and is essentially absent in npm1=0 patients.
Within unfit_for_intensive=1, the ven+aza ORR benefit is essentially abolished in tp53_mutation=1 patients.
Within unfit_for_intensive=1, the ven+aza ORR benefit is markedly attenuated in complex_karyotype=1 patients.

analyses.code: stratified ven+aza effect within unfit by npm1
analyses.result_summary: Unfit & npm1+: ORR 63.31% (ven+aza) vs 15.96% (control), diff=+0.474, p<1e-300. Unfit & npm1-: ORR 16.91% vs 16.45%, diff=+0.005, p=0.48. Massive positive effect modification by npm1 within unfit.

analyses.code: stratified ven+aza effect within unfit by tp53
analyses.result_summary: Unfit & tp53-: ORR 32.42% vs 16.19%, diff=+0.162, p=1.4e-142. Unfit & tp53+: ORR 17.66% vs 17.28%, diff=+0.004, p=0.87. tp53 mutation completely abolishes ven+aza benefit within unfit.

analyses.code: stratified ven+aza effect within unfit by complex_karyotype
analyses.result_summary: Unfit & CK-: ORR 33.00% vs 16.32%, diff=+0.167, p=9e-142. Unfit & CK+: ORR 18.49% vs 16.19%, diff=+0.023, p=0.11. Complex karyotype attenuates ven+aza benefit within unfit to a small non-significant difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a 3-way ven+aza × unfit_for_intensive × npm1_mutation positive interaction on objective_response.
ven+aza has no benefit in unfit_for_intensive=0 patients regardless of npm1 status.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ ven+aza * unfit * npm1 + tp53 + ck + ecog + age + secondary)
analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction beta=+2.16, z=20.6, p=2e-94. Effect of ven+aza (vs control) only manifests in unfit & npm1+.
Fit & npm1+: ven+aza ORR 15.67% vs 15.97% control (diff=-0.003, p=0.73). Confirms ven+aza inert in fit patients regardless of NPM1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 & npm1_mutation=1, tp53_mutation=1 abolishes the ven+aza response.

analyses.result_summary: Unfit & npm1+ & tp53-: ven+aza ORR 69.08% vs 15.69% control, diff=+0.534, p<1e-300. Unfit & npm1+ & tp53+: ven+aza ORR 18.84% vs 18.28%, diff=+0.006, p=0.94. tp53 mutation eliminates the response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 & npm1_mutation=1, complex_karyotype=1 abolishes the ven+aza response.

analyses.result_summary: Unfit & npm1+ & CK-: ven+aza ORR 71.65% vs 16.09% control, diff=+0.556, p<1e-300. Unfit & npm1+ & CK+: ORR 18.35% vs 15.20%, diff=+0.032, p=0.24. Complex karyotype eliminates the response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a very large objective_response benefit specifically in patients with unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 (the complete responder subgroup), with effect sizes >0.5 in absolute ORR difference.

analyses.code: stratified within unfit & npm1+ & tp53- & ck-
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup n=4531: ven+aza ORR 78.60% vs 15.83% control, diff=+0.628, chi-square p<1e-300. Multivariable-adjusted OR (within subgroup) = 21.4, p<1e-100. Largest treatment effect in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the (unfit & npm1+ & tp53- & ck-) subgroup, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no detectable effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: stratified in complement of responder subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Complement (n=45469): ven+aza ORR 16.40% vs 16.69% control, diff=-0.003, p=0.42. Confirms the entire ven+aza marginal benefit is concentrated in the responder subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Beyond ven+aza × (unfit, npm1, tp53, ck), no other treatment × binary-feature pair shows a strong (|beta|>0.2 with p<0.001) interaction on objective_response.

analyses.code: screen: for tx in treatments, mod in binaries: logit(resp ~ tx * mod)
analyses.result_summary: Strong interactions (p<0.001): ven+aza:unfit (+0.88, p=1e-78), ven+aza:npm1 (+1.05, p=5e-102), ven+aza:tp53 (-0.41, p=5e-7), ven+aza:complex_karyotype (-0.31, p=6e-6), enasidenib:sex_female (+0.31, p=2e-4), 7+3:complex_karyotype (-0.21, p=1.5e-3). No other treatment x binary interaction reaches |beta|>0.2 with p<0.001.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Beyond ven+aza, no treatment × continuous-feature interaction reaches a robust effect (Bonferroni-style threshold p<0.001) on objective_response.

analyses.code: screen: for tx in treatments, c in continuous: logit(resp ~ tx * z(c))
analyses.result_summary: Nominally significant (p<0.05) interactions: midostaurin:total_bilirubin (+0.07, p=0.02), midostaurin:sodium (-0.08, p=0.02), gilteritinib:alkphos (+0.10, p=0.001), gilteritinib:bun (-0.07, p=0.02), ivosidenib:sodium (-0.12, p=0.006), enasidenib:hemoglobin (-0.14, p=0.001), enasidenib:creatinine (+0.10, p=0.02), ven+aza:blast_pct (+0.07, p=0.004), ven+aza:weight_loss (+0.05, p=0.04), ven+aza:bun (-0.05, p=0.05). None survive a Bonferroni threshold (~6*18=108 tests, alpha=0.0005). Interpreted as noise.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_enasidenib has a positive effect in females (sex_female=1) and a negative effect in males (sex_female=0), independent of idh2 status.

analyses.code: stratified enasidenib effect by sex_female overall and within idh2+
analyses.result_summary: Overall: sex_female=0 enasidenib ORR 16.48% vs 18.81% (diff=-0.023, p=0.008); sex_female=1 ORR 21.17% vs 18.71% (diff=+0.025, p=0.012). Within idh2+: sex_female=0 diff=-0.030 p=0.36; sex_female=1 diff=+0.041 p=0.25. Interaction not driven by IDH2; no clear biological pattern, likely noise from multiple comparisons.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_ivosidenib reduces objective_response specifically in npm1_mutation=1 patients (negative ivosidenib × npm1 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: npm1+: ivosidenib ORR 21.49% vs 23.62% control, diff=-0.021, p=0.11. npm1-: diff +0.011, p=0.14. Interaction beta=-0.20, p=0.028. Effect very small and not robust to multiple comparisons.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_7plus3 has a negative effect on objective_response in complex_karyotype=1 patients (i.e., 7+3 is less effective in complex karyotype).

analyses.result_summary: ck=0: 7+3 ORR 19.49% vs 18.77% (diff=+0.007, p=0.06). ck=1: 7+3 ORR 15.55% vs 17.87% (diff=-0.023, p=0.008). Modest negative interaction (beta=-0.21, p=0.0015) — 7+3 may be less effective with complex karyotype, consistent with chemo-refractoriness in adverse cytogenetics.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the (unfit & npm1+ & tp53- & ck-) responder subgroup, the absolute ven+aza ORR benefit declines with worsening ECOG performance status but remains very large at every ECOG level.

analyses.code: stratified within responder core by ecog_ps
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0: ven+aza ORR 85.90% vs 19.35% (diff=+0.665, p<1e-100). ECOG 1: 77.16% vs 15.27% (diff=+0.619, p<1e-100). ECOG 2: 66.79% vs 9.80% (diff=+0.570, p<1e-50). Effect remains ~60-66 pp across all ECOG strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ven+aza adds no detectable benefit in fit (unfit_for_intensive=0) patients, even when they carry npm1_mutation=1 with favorable cytogenetics (tp53=0, ck=0).

analyses.result_summary: Fit & npm1+ & tp53- & ck- (n=6951): ven+aza ORR 15.78% vs 16.15% control, diff=-0.004, p=0.70. Confirms unfit_for_intensive=1 is a necessary condition for the ven+aza benefit, not just a marker for npm1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a fully adjusted model, the ven+aza × unfit × npm1 3-way interaction remains significant after controlling for tp53, complex_karyotype, ecog_ps, age, sex, wbc, blast %, albumin, weight loss, and crp; in addition, ven+aza × tp53 and ven+aza × complex_karyotype carry significant negative interactions.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ ven+aza * unfit * npm1 + ven+aza:tp53 + ven+aza:ck + covariates)
analyses.result_summary: ven+aza:unfit:npm1 beta=+2.20, p=2e-96. ven+aza:tp53 beta=-0.56, p=6e-11. ven+aza:complex_karyotype beta=-0.39, p=4e-8. All three interactions independently significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final best-supported subgroup hypothesis: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response only in patients meeting ALL of the following conditions — unfit_for_intensive=1, npm1_mutation=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0 — with an absolute ORR increase of approximately +0.63 (78.6% vs 15.8%); failure to meet any one of these four conditions reduces the effect to ~0.

For the targeted agents midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, and enasidenib, no biologically expected mutation-based responder subgroup is supported in this cohort (no significant FLT3-by-FLT3-inhibitor or IDH-by-IDH-inhibitor interactions on objective_response).
treatment_7plus3 provides modest benefit in complex_karyotype=0 patients but appears slightly disadvantageous in complex_karyotype=1 patients (negative 7+3 × complex_karyotype interaction).

analyses.code: Final 4-way subgroup confirmation: stratified analysis + adjusted logit
analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup (unfit & npm1+ & tp53- & ck-, n=4531): ven+aza ORR 78.60% vs 15.83% (diff=+0.628, adjusted OR=21.4, p<1e-100). Removing any one condition (fit, npm1-, tp53+, or ck+) reduces ven+aza diff to <+0.04 with non-significant p-values. Effect persists across ECOG 0/1/2 strata at +57 to +66 pp absolute.

midostaurin:flt3_itd p=0.19 (sign trends opposite expected), gilteritinib:flt3_itd p=0.32, ivosidenib:idh1 p=0.08 (sign opposite), enasidenib:idh2 p=0.79. None support targeted treatment-mutation matching for ORR in this dataset.
Interaction beta=-0.213, p=0.0015. ck=0 7+3 diff +0.007 (p=0.06); ck=1 7+3 diff -0.023 (p=0.008). Modest evidence 7+3 is less effective (slightly harmful) in complex karyotype.
